title: Developer Workflow
summary: The builder-facing layer of the atlas: how teams evaluate integration fit, structure their first implementation, and move from concept research into production planning.

bodySections.title: What builders need first
bodySections.body: Most teams do not begin with protocol internals. They begin with product requirements, data sensitivity, and user trust constraints. Developer Workflow exists to connect those practical questions to the right Arcium concepts.

bodySections.title: Decision points
bodySections.body: This category focuses on the decisions that shape adoption: which workloads are worth protecting, how application state changes when confidentiality is introduced, and what developer tradeoffs show up around UX, latency, and system design.

bodySections.title: Using the supporting material
bodySections.body: Read this category alongside the glossary and infrastructure content. The goal is not to memorize terms, but to build enough context to judge whether a workload belongs on Arcium and what a responsible integration path could look like.

description: A builder-facing guide to evaluating and planning Arcium integrations.
